opts: Try to switch from your backhand to your forehand first for a stronger shot here
Release your backhand immediately — the goalie is set for the forehand anyway
Pass the puck back out to a teammate waiting alone near the top of the slot
Skate behind the net first and look for a much better shooting angle around
Keep cycling the exact same way — something has to open up soon for a real shot in close
Stop the cycle — dump the puck deep, change lines, and come back with a new entry
Take a quick shot from deep in the corner area and hope the puck finds its way to the net
Pass all the way back to the point and start up a completely new play setup now
Nothing too specific yet — your four teammates already know how to handle a normal penalty kill and will figure it out together as it goes
Their specific penalty kill assignments — who covers the point, who covers the half-wall, and what the plan is if they get the puck
To skate as hard as they possibly can at every single puck carrier and never stop chasing until the two full minutes run out on the clock
To surround the puck carrier at all times with all four skaters so there is no chance at all for a clean pass or a real shot on the goalie
Commit hard to the puck carrier on the left and try poking the puck off their stick before they set up a play
Stay in the middle — take away the pass to the cutter and force the shot. Your goalie handles the shot.
Chase the cutter all the way to the back post so they can't tap in a pass coming across the crease
Back up all the way to your own crease and stand right next to the goalie to cover any rebound
Wind up a big slap shot right off the faceoff draw to try to extend your lead early in the period
Control the puck, cycle below the dots, use every second — only shoot on a high-quality chance
Dump the puck hard into the far corner and chase after it as a group with all three of your forwards
Send it all the way back to your own zone to play it safe and kill as much of the clock as you can
Force the pass to the left winger — they are the first option every single time
Pass to the right winger — the forechecker has already cut off the left
Carry the puck out yourself up the middle and try to beat the forechecker
Rim the puck around the boards hard and hope your winger can pick it up
Try to hold the puck and fight through all the pressure until a lane opens
Drop a back pass to your center — they have time and space and you don't
Shoot it right through the defender and hope for a deflection or bounce in
Dump the puck deep into the corner and send all the forwards in to chase
Top right — shoot high over their shoulder on the same side they are leaning hard toward
Low left — the opposite side of where they're leaning. They can't recover in time.
Right at their chest — power and accuracy will beat almost any goalie cleanly
Wait a second for the goalie to reset their position before you decide to release
Go immediately — you are fresh and your team really needs the extra help on the ice right now as play continues
Wait until the puck is dead, there's a stoppage, or your teammates are safely at the bench and can't be caught short
Jump onto the ice the moment your teammate steps off the bench, even if they're still deep in the play and skating hard
Go whenever you feel rested and ready to jump in on the play, no matter if the puck is live or dead right then
Wait until all three of your forwards are together in neutral zone first
Go immediately — the 3-on-1 window closes fast as the other team gets back
Regroup back at your own blue line first and reload as a group again
Dump the puck in deep and head straight to the bench to change now
Watch the puck carrier closely and wait for the pass to actually happen first
Step in front of the anticipated pass lane — intercept it before it arrives
Skate straight into the corner and try to pressure the puck carrier hard
Back up all the way and hug the crease right next to your own goalie
Ignore the attacker completely and trust that your partner will just follow them over
Call switch and take the attacker coming to your side so your partner can recover
Call for the referee to whistle a penalty on the attacker for crossing in front
Back all the way up to the crease and let the attacker set up wherever they want to go
Stay on the left side of the ice where the puck is and help your teammates battle along the boards for possession
Move immediately to the right side — the space is there and the D will have to follow you or leave you open
Head straight to the bench for a line change while your teammates still hold the puck on the left side of the ice
Stand in front of the opposing net to screen their goalie and wait there for a shot or rebound from a teammate
Nothing at all — chasing pucks is actually a really smart PK strategy that you should use the whole time
Chasing breaks the box — gaps open up and the power play gets into the dangerous areas you were protecting
Chasing wears out the power play much faster and forces them to rush every single pass they try to make
Chasing makes the power play players hesitate and second-guess every pass they make around the outside of the box
Pass to a winger right away — passing first on a rush is always the right play to make
Shoot — you're at full speed and the goalie has to set against a moving shooter
Pull up at the top of the circles and stickhandle to wait for your wingers
Dump the puck into the zone and reset the rush with your line on the way back
Force a hard pass to one of the covered wingers and hope they can somehow win the battle along the boards in the end
Reverse behind the net — the forechecker on the left has to follow, which buys time for the center to get back
Carry the puck straight out yourself and try to skate right through the forechecker coming hard from your left side
Shoot the puck all the way down the ice and take the icing whistle to get a fresh faceoff in your own zone now
Skate hard out at the defenseman at the point so they cannot get off a clean shot through the screen at the net
Move the screener out of the crease — your goalie can't see the shot and that's more dangerous than the shot itself
Stay in the closest passing lane so that no pass from the point can slide through to anyone in the slot or the crease area
Back up all the way to your own crease and help your goalie block any shot that might come from the point right now here
Wait patiently for the play to develop on its own before you decide what to do with your positioning in the offensive zone for the shift
Drive hard to the net immediately — the opposing center just spent all their energy on the draw and the lane to the net may be briefly open
Skate all the way over to the corner and see what kind of play develops down low before you decide to commit to any real position in the zone
Call loudly for a pass from the defenseman at the point so you can get the puck back and try to set up a play in the high slot on this shift
The teammate in front of the net — they're in the best scoring position right on the doorstep
The one at the half-wall — they have the open lane right now and can shoot immediately
Hold the puck yourself and wait for a much cleaner option to open up in the zone
Pass it back to the point defenseman and just reset the offensive zone play again
Nobody covers it — you handle both the half-wall and the corner
Your D partner rotates to cover the corner behind you the rotation
Your winger on the other side skates across and trust the play to develop
You stay in both spots at the same time somehow during the same shift
Stay exactly where you are and trust that your defenseman will figure out the play on their own without any help from you
Skate to the wall hard, using your speed to lose the forechecker for a split second — make the D's decision easy
Don't break to the wall until the forechecker has already left your area and given up on the whole chase already
Stand completely still in your spot and call out loudly for the puck to really try to get your D's attention on the play
Shoot — you have the puck and a decent angle, so take the shot before anything changes
Pass to the slot player — the higher danger location is worth the slight coverage risk
Hold onto the puck and skate around waiting for an even better chance to open up eventually
Pass all the way back to the point and let the defenseman set up a new play from there now
Get to the puck first and try to skate it all the way out of the zone yourself before the opponent can close on you now
Get your body between the opponent and the puck — inside position wins this battle even if you get there second
Poke at the puck with your stick from a distance and try to knock it loose without getting into a hard physical battle
Call loudly for your goalie to come out and get the puck while you block the opposing player from reaching the front of net
Carry the puck straight up the middle every single time since you already have the extra man advantage anyway
Use the extra man advantage: hold the puck until one PK player commits, then exploit what they've given up
Dump the puck deep into the corner and send all five skaters in hard to chase and recover the puck again
Skate all the way back into your own zone and regroup over and over until the PK gets very tired out
Stand still right where you are and call for the puck loudly so the puck carrier knows exactly where you are on the ice and can pass to you
Skate into open space — cut to the net, move to a seam. Make yourself available AND drag your defender out of the puck carrier's area.
Go help the puck carrier in the battle for the puck by skating right into the pile to try to get the puck loose for the team at last
Skate out to the blue line right away so you are ready to make a breakout pass if the other team gets the puck back and clears it
Stay exactly where you are in the offensive zone and wait to see if the turnover really happens
Start transitioning early — turn toward your own zone before the turnover is complete. Get a head start.
Skate straight to your own bench and get off for a quick line change before the turnover actually happens
Head to the nearest corner in the zone and wait there patiently for the puck to come loose to you soon
Force the pass through the defender — your teammate is still in the slot and you can see part of them right now
Find a better option — a covered pass through a defender is high-risk. Look for the winger or the point.
Hold onto the puck yourself and just skate around the zone looking for another option to open up soon on the play
Take a shot yourself from right where you are standing instead of trying to make any kind of pass at all here
Yes — always pinch hard and join every single attack so you can keep the puck deep in the zone longer for team
Only if the other winger or center is positioned to cover the blue line behind you — otherwise hold the line
Yes — pinch down every single time your winger touches the puck, no matter who is covering the blue line behind
No — never pinch down from the blue line under any circumstances at all because it is just always too risky to try
